summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of an all‑time great 1985 show – seamless second set, stellar setlist (Big Boy Pete, She Belongs to Me, Walking the Dog, The Other One) and superb Brent‑era keyboards.
review: Fans rave about this recording’s pristine soundboard mix, calling it crisp, superbly balanced and a standout CM transfer. The performance is described as one of the best of the ’80s era – a tight first set and an almost flawless second set with memorable moments like Big Boy Pete, She Belongs to Me, Walking the Dog and a flowing The Other One → Wharf Rat → Playing reprise sequence.

ai_rating.rationale: All detailed reviews (5+) praise both the musical performance and the recording quality; no substantive negatives were raised.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, balanced mix praised by multiple reviewers; noted as a superb CM transfer with no significant drop‑outs or muddiness. Only occasional comment about Jerry’s raspy vocal tone, not a recording flaw.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as hot and energetic; second set seamless and even more amped, with creative song pairings (Shakedown > Crazy Fingers, The Other One > Wharf Rat > Playing reprise) that kept momentum high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice a bit raspy/“smoked” on this night but guitar work praised; overall performance hailed as exceptional.
band_member_comments.Phil: "Going to kick ass" – bass energy highlighted and praised throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s vocal entry noted at the start of the concert (BBP reference).
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland era keyboards lauded; mixing board work by Healy in ’85 singled out as superb.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum segment during She Belongs To Me highlighted positively; overall tight, crisp rhythm section.
